Petition To Invalidate Irrevocable Trust, To Invalidate Trust Amendment, For Return Of Trust Property, For Intentional Interference
Off Calendar Jul-19-2021 Continued To Nov-29-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer McGuire, via ZOOM, for Anthony Sabada, Petitioner; Nancy Lofdahl, via ZOOM, for Berta Quezada, Respondent.

The court begins by asking counsel for an update regarding settlement discuissions. Ms. Mcguire reports that part of the case resolved via mediation. The court then asks counsel how they wish to proceed. Both counsel request that the matter be set for trial.

The matter is set for trial in the department of the Presiding Judge (206) on November 29, 2021 at 9:30 a.m. Order for the same signed in open court. Counsel is advised to monitor the court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the order and notice of trial.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: B. Quesada. Not reported.

Petition To Invalidate Irrevocable Trust, To Invalidate Trust Amendment, For Return Of Trust Property, For Intentional Interference
Off Calendar May-17-2021 Continued To Jul-19-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Invalidate Irrevocable Trust, To Invalidate Trust Amendment, For Return Of Trust Property, For Intentional Interference
Off Calendar Mar-01-2021 Continued To May-17-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard concurrently:
PES-19-303300 The Estate of Juan W. Sabada and
PTR-20-303542 In the matter of the Cantillano Irrevocable Trust

Appearances:
Jennifer McGuire (via Zoom) for and with Anthony Cantillano Sabada (via (Zoom).
Nancy Lofdahl (via Zoom) for Berta Quezada.

Ms. Lofdahl stated the 850 petition in the estate case can be resolved with a half day mediation. Ms. McGuire stated she would like a single mediation for all matters and would like to see the 850 petition granted. Ms. Lofdahl stated her client opposes the 850 petition, that the assets are not her client's, that her client was holding the assets for her brother, that none of the children are interested in it and it should go back to the estate for the one child in the estate matter. The Court noted Ms. McGuire's position that no written objections were filed. Ms. Lofdahl stated her client does object, that she is making oral objections now and the best way to resolve is mediation. Ms. McGuire stated Ms. Lofdahl has already conceded that the money should go back to the estate, that she would be happy to mediate both matters, and it would save time and money to deal with both matters at once instead of mediating on just one. Ms. Lofdahl stated that she can communicate with Ms. McGuire regarding her client's fees for helping her brother, that her client assisted her brother with his real properties and his court cases to clarify his name, provided tax support, provided assistance with his immigration status/name correction to collect his pension, that he was dependent on her and she should get compensation from the sale of these real properties.

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than May 3, 2021. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by May 10, 2021. The petitions in both the Estate and Trust cases are continued to May 17, 2021, and the Parties are to return on May 17, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: S. Le. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Invalidate Irrevocable Trust, To Invalidate Trust Amendment, For Return Of Trust Property, For Intentional Interference
Off Calendar Sep-14-2020 Continued To Oct-05-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Demurrer To Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendment, For Return Of Trust Property, For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance, And For An Accounting
Off Calendar Aug-20-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer McGuire, Esq., counsel for Petitioner, Anthony Cantillano [by video conferencing]; Nancy E. Lofdahl, Esq., counsel for and with Bertina Quezada, respondent [both by video conferencing, separately appearing].

The Clerk calls the calendar; counsel state their appearances. The Court notes that the tentative ruling was adverse to Ms. Quezada and invites Ms. Lofdahl to adress the tentative ruling. Ms. Lofdahl contests the allegations of the late discovery rule, noting that the petition fails to allege facts to support why the discovery was late, given that the various deeds ultimately conveying title to Ms. Quezada were duly recorded. In the absence of a late discovery allegation, the applicable statute of limitation would have expired and the Petition dismissed.

Ms. McGuire responds. The Court notes that while there may be a basis for a statute of limitiations defense, it is not entirely clear that the defense is applicable at this time and would not support demurrer. Ms. Lofdahl replies, emphasizing the absence of late discovery elements which the Court finds to be premature and, perhaps, a more restrictive reading of the general purpose of demurrer. Counsel submit.

The Court affirms the tentative ruling, overruling the demurrer. Ms. McGuire is to prepare the order. After discussion, Ms. Quezada's opposition to the Petition will be due within 10 days from the date of this hearing.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)
